aliases: The Short One in the Tree

visual_dna.ethnicity: 1st Century Jewish — chief tax collector for a major city
visual_dna.skin_tone: olive, soft from indoor wealthy life — he does not labor in the sun
visual_dna.hair_texture: short, well-kept — he can afford good grooming
visual_dna.beard_style: short, well-trimmed — prosperity in the details
visual_dna.eye_color: brown, sharp and calculating — then shocked, then joyful
visual_dna.height_build: notably short — Luke 19:3 explicitly states he was short and couldn't see over the crowd. This is the entire mechanism of the scene.
distinguishing_marks: short stature — explicitly stated, must be visible in casting and framing
fine quality Roman-influenced robes — chief tax collector wealth is visible
gold accessories — rings, belt detail — the visible signs of his collaboration with Rome
a wealthy powerful man in a sycamore tree because he had no other option — dignity surrendered to desperation

wardrobe_by_arc.tree_scene: fine quality robes now crumpled from climbing a tree, gold accessories, fine sandals — a wealthy man in an undignified position
wardrobe_by_arc.post_encounter: same clothing — but the posture has completely changed

voice_profile.register: higher tenor — nervous energy in the tree scene, then sudden bold declaration
voice_profile.pace: quick, anxious before — then deliberate and generous after
voice_profile.accent_note: Jericho Aramaic — a major trading city accent
speech_mannerisms: nothing before the encounter — he is watching, not speaking
'Look, Lord! Here and now I give half of my possessions to the poor' — the transformation is immediate and excessive
'If I have cheated anybody out of anything, I will pay back four times the amount' — the language of a man who knows exactly what he's done
voice_profile.notes: The theological point of the scene is that Jesus initiates — 'I must stay at your house today.' Zacchaeus did not invite him. He only wanted to see. Jesus invited himself.

emotional_arc.opening: Wealthy, powerful, despised. The most hated man in Jericho. So isolated in his wealth that he climbs a tree alone to see a passing teacher.
emotional_arc.midpoint: Jesus stops. In a crowd of hundreds. And calls his name. Looks up. 'Zacchaeus, come down immediately. I must stay at your house today.'
emotional_arc.climax: The crowd's disgust — 'He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.' Then Zacchaeus stands up and announces radical generosity.
emotional_arc.resolution: 'Today salvation has come to this house, because this man, too, is a son of Abraham.' Jesus names what happened.

evangelical_baptist.notes: The salvation narrative — 'Today salvation has come to this house.' Clear gospel moment.
evangelical_baptist.marketing_angle: Jesus walked past thousands of people that day. He stopped under one tree. And called one name.

catholic.notes: The encounter of grace — Jesus's initiative of love, Zacchaeus's response
catholic.marketing_angle: He didn't invite Jesus. Jesus invited himself. That's grace.

black_church.notes: The collaborator redeemed — his radical generosity as a sign of genuine transformation
black_church.marketing_angle: He worked for the oppressor. One encounter changed not just his heart but his checkbook.

hispanic_christian.notes: The encounter that transforms — the immediacy of the conversion resonates
hispanic_christian.marketing_angle: El momento en que Jesús lo vio — y su vida nunca fue la misma

secular_curious.notes: The most visually comedic and theologically rich scene in Luke — the mechanics are brilliant
secular_curious.marketing_angle: The most powerful man in the room couldn't see over the crowd. So he climbed a tree. And someone stopped under it.

youth_gen_z.notes: The person no one liked who got seen by the one person who mattered
youth_gen_z.marketing_angle: He was the most hated man in the city. Jesus walked past everyone else and stopped at his tree.

jewish_american.notes: The tax collector who worked for Rome — 'a son of Abraham' reclaims his Jewish identity
jewish_american.marketing_angle: Jesus calls him 'a son of Abraham' — reclaiming his identity that his collaboration had cost him

women.marketing_angle: The man everyone wrote off. The one Jesus refused to walk past.

men.notes: Wealthy, successful, empty — the man who had everything and was completely alone
men.marketing_angle: He had everything. He was completely alone. One conversation changed everything.

audience_targeting.modern_parallel: The rich man everyone hated — including himself — until one encounter changed everything
audience_targeting.modern_parallel_detail: He didn't ask for salvation. He asked for a view. Jesus had bigger plans.

ai_generation.consistency_seed_prompt: 1st century wealthy Jewish chief tax collector age 40-50, notably short stature, olive skin soft from indoor life, thinning dark hair, short trimmed beard, fine quality Roman-influenced robes, gold accessories, perched awkwardly in a sycamore fig tree above a Jericho street crowd, expression shifting from anxious effort to shocked joy, bright midday Jericho sun, cinematic photoreal, 8k
ai_generation.negative_prompt: tall build, poor clothing, cartoonish, European features, obviously villainous expression
ai_generation.midjourney_style_params: cinematic photoreal, bright Jericho midday sun, wide street establishing then intimate close on face
ai_generation.firefly_prompt: Photorealistic scene of a notably short, wealthy 1st century Jewish man, age 40-50, in fine robes and gold accessories, awkwardly perched in a sycamore fig tree above a crowded Jericho street, expression of surprised joy as he looks down at someone calling his name, bright midday Mediterranean sunlight, crowd below, cinematic framing from below looking up at him
reference_films: The Chosen TV series — for tax collector world and character complexity
ai_generation.color_grading_notes: Jericho: bright, commercial, wealthy — one of the richest cities in Judea. The scene should feel prosperous and hot. His transformation changes his expression but not his clothes — same man, different heart.

production_notes: The tree shot is the signature image of this scene. Frame it: wealthy powerful man in fine robes, crammed into a tree like a boy. Then Jesus stops directly underneath and looks up. The humor and the holiness coexist.
